senior_junior_culture.summary: Official clubs and councils create cross-batch interaction, but recent independent evidence on mentorship, hierarchy and fresher inclusion is limited.
positive_themes: Clubs, councils and technical teams provide interaction channels
negative_themes: Insufficient recent independent evidence about hierarchy or mentoring consistency

academic_culture.summary: IIT Hyderabad uses a distinctive fractal academic system. Student accounts describe continuous assessment, branch-dependent attendance rules, project opportunities and meaningful workload variation.
positive_themes: Fractal course structure can reduce a single common exam peak
Academic flexibility through minors/double majors where rules permit
negative_themes: Workload and grading experience varies by branch and instructor

hostel_reality.summary: Official sources report individual rooms, radiant cooling, Wi-Fi/LAN and residential UG life. Recent videos support strong infrastructure, while mess satisfaction and block-to-block cooling experience vary.
positive_themes: Single rooms in official current description
negative_themes: Mess quality is subjective and conflicting
Some rooms may experience cooling or maintenance variation

club_and_campus_life.summary: The institute publishes a broad technical, cultural, literary, service and entrepreneurship ecosystem, including Elan & nVision. Student sources also describe active clubs and inter-hostel events.
negative_themes: Current recruitment cycles and selection fairness are not uniformly public

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: Official placement and internship sources show strong participation by software, finance, consulting, semiconductor and engineering employers. Peer accounts indicate active DSA and non-core preparation, but branch eligibility varies and public company-level rules are incomplete.
negative_themes: Company eligibility and role-level data are not fully public

locality_and_student_life.summary: The campus is a large, self-contained residential site in Kandi/Sangareddy. Official routes connect it to Hyderabad, but student reports consistently note that malls and central-city activities usually require substantial travel.
positive_themes: Road access toward Hyderabad technology corridors
negative_themes: Approximately one-hour access to many city destinations
Immediate locality is less urban than central Hyderabad

overall_satisfaction.summary: Available evidence supports modern infrastructure, strong academics and a substantial club/recruiter ecosystem. Common trade-offs are academic intensity, mess variation, non-urban location and branch-dependent placement access. No numeric satisfaction score is justified.
